summary: Large fighter decomp PR for src/melee/ft/ft_0D14.c, primarily matching the Kinoko/mushroom-related common fighter logic. The decomp.dev report credited 104 new full matches in main/melee/ft/ft_0D14, +10504 matched bytes, and global matched code moving to 27.16% (+0.27%). The patch also added motion-variable layouts, corrected several prototypes and data types, and exposed ftCo_80090AC0 from DamageIce for use outside its C file.
what_changed: Expanded src/melee/ft/ft_0D14.c from a mostly stub/placeholder file into roughly 2050 lines of matched C.

Added matched common fighter functions for Super Mushroom and Poison Mushroom application/end handling, including Fighter_SuperMushroomApply, Fighter_SuperMushroomEnd, Fighter_PoisonMushroomApply, and Fighter_PoisonMushroomEnd.

Implemented many Kinoko motion-state handlers and transition helpers, including ground/air start and end variants such as ftCo_KinokoGiantStart_Anim, ftCo_KinokoGiantStartAir_Coll, ftCo_KinokoSmallStart_Anim, and ftCo_KinokoSmallEnd_Coll.
Added shared static inline helpers in ft_0D14.c for repeated motion setup patterns such as ftCo_800D170C_inline, ftCo_800D1BA0_inline, ftCo_800D1FFC_inline, and ftCo_800D2490_inline.
Added ftCommon_MotionVars union members in src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/types.h: common, mushroom, unk_800D2890, unk_800D331C, unk_800D34E0, unk_800D3680, unk_deadleft, and unk_deadup.
Changed Fighter_804D6508 and Fighter_804D650C from UNK_T/int* to u8* in fighter.c and fighter.h, and adjusted ftCo_Guard.c local pointer typing accordingly.
Promoted ftCo_80090AC0 from a static declaration in ftCo_DamageIce.c to a header declaration in ftCo_DamageIce.h with signature void ftCo_80090AC0(Fighter*).
Refined ftCo_800CBAC4 declaration in ft_0C88.h from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to void ftCo_800CBAC4(Fighter_GObj*, ftCommon_MotionState, Vec3*, int).
Updated config/GALE01/symbols.txt metadata for ftCo_804D3CD8 and ftCo_804D3CE0 from generic 0x8 objects to local string data of sizes 0x7 and 0x5.
smart_moves: Matched pairs of very similar ground/air and giant/small mushroom routines by factoring repeated setup into local static inline helpers, which helps preserve duplicated codegen shape while avoiding copy-paste divergence.
Used ftCommon_MotionVars field overlays for state-specific callback storage and temporary fields, especially mv.co.mushroom.x0/x4/x8, rather than forcing incorrect global struct names.
Tightened prototypes only where diff evidence supported it, replacing UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS signatures with concrete Fighter_GObj, Fighter*, ftCommon_MotionState, Vec3*, int, and bool/int return types.

Corrected global data pointer width to u8* for Fighter_804D6508/Fighter_804D650C, allowing byte-indexed access patterns like &Fighter_804D650C[temp_ret] and M2C_FIELD byte loads to better reflect assembly.
Preserved compiler-sensitive matching controls with #pragma push, #pragma dont_inline on, and #pragma pop around functions where inlining behavior mattered.
Recorded sdata objects as strings in symbols.txt when identified, improving data typing for later decompilation around ftCo_804D3CD8 and ftCo_804D3CE0.
decomp_lessons: For common fighter status files, matching one behavior cluster often requires simultaneously updating ft_0D14.c, ft_0D14.h, ftCommon motion-variable overlays, external prototypes, and global data declarations.

State-specific motion variable unions should be expanded with offset comments and padding arrays when exact field meanings are still unknown; this supports matching without overnaming speculative semantics.

Ground/air motion transitions commonly share most setup but call different transition helpers such as ft_8008403C versus ft_80082C74 and different state IDs; local inline helpers are a useful matching tactic for these pairs.

Callbacks embedded in Fighter fields and motion variables, such as take_dmg_cb and mv.co.mushroom.x0/x4, are central to matching status transitions and should be typed as callable event/function pointers when evidence supports it.
Pointer element type changes can affect unrelated files' matching percentages; this PR improved ft_0D14 heavily but caused small reported regressions in several ftCo_Guard unmatched functions.
Data-symbol metadata matters: distinguishing strings from generic objects in symbols.txt can be part of a successful code-match PR.
naming_conventions: Externally meaningful item-effect entry points use Fighter_* names, e.g. Fighter_SuperMushroomApply, Fighter_SuperMushroomEnd, Fighter_PoisonMushroomApply, Fighter_PoisonMushroomEnd.
Common fighter motion callbacks use ftCo_<StateName>_<Phase> naming where known, e.g. ftCo_KinokoGiantStart_Anim, ftCo_KinokoGiantStart_Coll, ftCo_KinokoSmallStartAir_Anim.
Unknown-address functions in common fighter code retain ftCo_800Dxxxx names; local/helper functions can use fn_800Dxxxx or static inline ftCo_800Dxxxx_inline when no semantic name is established.
Motion-variable union members use semantic names when known, such as mushroom and common, and unk_<address> names when tied to an unknown function/address.
Struct fields retain offset-based xNN names with comments like /* fp+2340 */ when semantics are not yet established.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used #pragma dont_inline around selected routines, including mushroom apply/animation handlers, to match compiler inlining decisions.
Introduced local static inline setup functions to make repeated C source patterns match multiple related assembly routines without generating extra callable functions.
Used small dummy stack arrays such as u8 _[8] in some Anim handlers to influence stack frame shape.
Changed pointer types to byte pointers where the assembly indicated byte-addressed indexing and u8 field extraction.
Maintained exact motion-state change flags and floating constants in Fighter_ChangeMotionState calls, including 0x90, 0x91, 0xC4C5080, 0xC4C5081, 0.0F, and 1.0F.
Added union overlays with explicit padding expressions such as u8 pad_x0[0x6c - 0x40] and u8 pad_x44[0x6c - 0x44] to target known field offsets.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the slice.
Automated decomp.dev report showed 104 new matches, 3 improvements in unmatched functions, and 4 small regressions in unmatched ftCo_Guard functions.
Automated report highlighted major new ft_0D14 matches including ftCo_800D3158, ftCo_800D331C, ftCo_800D15D0, ftCo_800D34E0, Fighter_SuperMushroomApply, and Fighter_PoisonMushroomApply.
Automated report noted ftCo_800D3680 reached 54.93% and ftCo_800D40B8 reached 65.67%, so not all touched ft_0D14 functions were fully matched.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that finish ftCo_800D3680 or ftCo_800D40B8 after this partial match.
Search for subsequent renames or semantic typing of ftCommon_MotionVars.mushroom x0/x4/x8 fields.
Check whether the small ftCo_Guard regressions from the Fighter_804D650C u8* change were later recovered.
Look for later PRs that give semantic names to ftCo_804D3CD8 and ftCo_804D3CE0 strings.
Search for PRs that continue matching other ranges adjacent to ft_0D14 or common fighter item-effect states.
